title: zoysia matrella var. tenuifolia
intro: Zoysia matrella var. tenuifolia, also known as Japanese mint or Korean mint, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Zoysia matrella var. tenuifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or using a solvent to extract the active compounds.
